mentions.note: Replacing copper with fiber in data centers nationwide. Prospects terrific — own the business. Should hope it comes down to buy; I wasn't bullish enough in club meeting.

Won $6B Meta fiber optic cable contract; rallied 15% on news; CEO Weeks says hyperscalers will be biggest customers; fiber superior to copper for data center linking; upside from other hyperscalers and potential future chip applications.

February's second-best performer (+45.7%); Cramer trust position up 96% in 5 months; printing money on fiber optic equipment for AI data centers; $6B Meta deal + strong guidance drove late-January rally.
Owns double in charitable trust; wait for pullback before adding. Stock vulnerable to further 10% downside in this market; all big movers at risk.
Fiber company replacing copper in data-center infrastructure; best pure-play on fiber transition; top-three ranking for AI infrastructure build.
Fiber company taking over what copper was in data centers; best-positioned in fiber infrastructure play; preferred over traditional copper exposure.
Non-stop decline amid memory stock rout; caught in collateral damage of Google memory-killer narrative. Awaiting fundamental stabilization.
Materials and optical fiber supplier benefiting from hardware/data center buildout.
Caller up 90% since December; trades 55x P/E but Cramer owns for charitable trust; glass and fiber taking over data centers—next-year estimates too low.
Fiber optic glass supplier for iPhone and data center buildout. Visited factory with Tim Cook; critical to AI infrastructure.
Parabolic run led to −9% Icarus moment despite two new major clients; healthy correction; pro move is 'schnitzeling' (sell portions on rallies, rebuy on 5–7% dips).
Glass & fiber company; analyst meeting Wednesday could highlight multi-billion dollar data-center contracts. Could move stock higher.
Provides fiber optics connecting data center infrastructure equipment.

Stock quadrupled in 12 months; Cramer owns in trust. Nvidia partnership driving 10x optical capacity expansion, multi-billion-dollar investment. Targeting $20B→$30B→$40B revenue. Made-in-America manufacturing bet on AI infrastructure.

Optical fiber critical infrastructure for AI data centers. Nvidia partnership multibillion-dollar commitment, tenfold capacity expansion, 3,000 jobs. Targets $20B (2026), $30B (2028), $40B (2030) revenue—exceptional for 175-year-old company.
Excellent long-term company yielding 5%+ facing tough times; dividend sustainability questionable.
Parabolic move; down 7% insufficient pullback. Needs more decline before re-entry.
Fiber displaces copper in data center for speed, cybersecurity, corrosion resistance; Nvidia invested $3.2B stake; CEO Wendell Weeks' conviction crystal clear.
Better positioned than AAOI for data center buildout exposure with superior fundamentals. Preferred choice in uncertain market despite recent valuation pressures.
Charitable Trust trimmed some shares today to lock in a big win, but remains a large holder overall.
